iti18:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti18:2.1:
“One thing, monks, when arising in the world, arises for the detriment of many,
for the unhappiness of many, for the detriment & unhappiness of many beings, both human & divine.
iti18:2.4:
When the Saṅgha is split, there are arguments with one another,
there is abuse of one another, ganging up on one another, abandoning of one another.
iti18:2.5:
There those with little confidence [in the teaching] lose all confidence,
while some of those who are confident become otherwise.”
Doomed for an eon
iti18:3.2: one who has split the Saṅgha.
iti18:3.4: he’s barred from safety from bondage.
iti18:3.5: Having split a Saṅgha in concord,
iti18:3.6: he cooks for an eon in hell.
